method: multi_model_grounding_audit — 4 models × 25 queries × 10 languages, citations classified by source origin

classification_method.sanctioned: Domain match against US OFAC SDN CSV (treasury.gov/ofac/downloads/sdn.csv), EU Consolidated Financial Sanctions CSV (webgate.ec.europa.eu), UK OFSI CSV (ofsistorage.blob.core.windows.net). Financial sanctions only — EU broadcast bans excluded.
classification_method.government: .gov.ru TLD + known government domains (mid.ru and subdomains, kremlin.ru, cbr.ru, studyinrussia.ru)

key_findings: Sanctioned media contamination rate is 0.1% (5 citations, all RIA Novosti via Perplexity).
7.6% of all grounding citations route through Russian-origin (.ru/.su) sources.
Russian government domains (mid.ru, cbr.ru, .gov.ru) account for 1.1% of citations.
Perplexity has highest Russian-origin rate (11.0%), followed by GPT-4o (16.8%, small sample), Gemini (7.4%), Claude (4.3%).
GEC-documented proxy sites accessible when searched by name (74 citations in targeted probes) but not in neutral queries.
The contamination pathway is institutional, not propagandistic: universities (cfuv.ru), banks (cbr.ru), telecom guides normalize Crimea as Russian through infrastructure, not narrative.
notes: Gemini 2.5 Flash returns vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com redirect URLs that were resolved via HTTP 302 to get actual source domains.
